column1: # InfoTerm custom partition table (4 MB flash)
# Replaces the stock huge_app.csv to grow nvs: with ~250 individual
# Preferences keys (32 MQTT slots x 5 fields
# across three never-cleaned-up key generations (cfg_*
# the stock 20 KB nvs partition was running out of free pages
# Arduino core silently erases+reformats the whole nvs partition on the next
# boot when that happens (esp32-hal-misc.c calls nvs_flash_erase() on
# ESP_ERR_NVS_NO_FREE_PAGES) - this was the real cause of settings loss.
# CRITICAL constraint discovered the hard way: PlatformIO's Arduino-framework
# upload ALWAYS writes framework-arduinoespressif32/tools/partitions/
# boot_app0.bin to the hardcoded physical address 0xE000 on every single
# `pio run -t upload`

column1: # is no working override for it (board_upload.ota_partition_offset does NOT

column2: confirmed by testing). An earlier version of this table put

column1: # nvs across 0xB000-0x40000
# corrupted whatever nvs entries physically landed in that ~8KB window
# reproducibly wiping general settings + the first few MQTT slots on every
# reflash (NOT on a plain reset - only on upload). Fix: otadata is placed
# EXACTLY at 0xE000 (its conventional home anyway
# content belongs)
# ever occupies the 0xE000-0x10000 window. 0x9000-0xE000 (20 KB) is
# deliberately left as an unallocated gap - nothing else in the observed
# upload command writes there
# spiffs is kept (not just dead weight): the WebGUI is fully inline HTML/CSS/JS
# and never served template files from flash (the old INFOTERM_USE_LITTLEFS
# template path was dead code and was removed in 0.9.14)
# StorageBackupRuntime.inc / WebGuiRuntime.inc mount LittleFS directly to
# store the config Backup/Restore JSON file - removing spiffs would break
# that feature.
# board_upload.offset_address in platformio.ini MUST match app0's offset
# here (0x50000) - PlatformIO does not derive the upload address from this
# file for the Arduino framework (see platformio.ini comment).
# app0/app1 (0.9.13): true dual-slot OTA. Confirmed live on hardware that a
# single ota_0-only layout cannot support OTA at all - ESP-IDF's
# esp_flash_erase_region() unconditionally aborts() rather than let you erase
# the app partition your code is currently executing from (Update.write()
# crashed immediately on the very first sector erase). A second app
# partition is not an optional safety nicety
column2: it is the only way OTA can

column1: # function on this chip: Update.begin() writes into whichever of app0/app1
# is NOT currently running
column2: and otadata then flips the active slot. Shrunk
